item.title: 円安･高金利は危機でなく転機､市場を羅針盤にあるべき経済の姿語ろう
item.description: 市場の変化を単なるリスクとして恐れるのではなく、日本経済の新たな成長戦略を描くための羅針盤として生かせるか。

item.title: India Micro-Caps Defy Hazy Macro With Breakout Signal
item.description: The Bloomberg India Micro Cap Index has formed a technical signal watched by traders.

item.title: Lingyi Seeks to Raise Up to $1.1 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
item.description: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing that may raise as much as HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion).

item.title: Australia LNG Union Reaches Deal to End Strikes at Inpex Plant
item.description: Unions in Australia and Japan’s Inpex Corp. reached an agreement to end strikes at the Ichthys liquefied natural gas export facility after weeks of industrial action.

item.title: Top Fintech GCash Owner Secures Approval for Philippine IPO
item.description: The company behind GCash, the Philippines’ top fintech firm, has secured approval for a potential initial public offering, its key shareholder Globe Telecom Inc. said.

item.title: SpaceX Drew $800 Million From Korean Investors on Trading Debut
item.description: South Korean retail investors snapped up almost $800 million of SpaceX shares on the stock’s first day of trading, underscoring strong demand for Elon Musk’s company after local investors were shut out from the world’s largest initial public offering.

item.title: General Atlantic in Talks to Lead Funding for China’s Kling AI
item.description: Kuaishou Technology is in discussions with General Atlantic to lead a first round of financing for its video AI arm, seeking to secure a major US backer ahead of an initial public offering.

item.title: Australia Must Gird for Shock-Prone Financial System, RBA Warns
item.description: Australian institutions need to be ready to respond to a more shock-prone financial system as a strained geopolitical environment reshapes financial and economic linkages, a senior Reserve Bank official said Wednesday.

item.title: HSBC Expects Over $100 Million Gains From Using Google AI Tools
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc said it will use Alphabet Inc.’s Google Cloud to roll out artificial intelligence across its global operations, including through projects that can each generate more than $100 million in extra revenue or savings.

item.title: Bond Rally Fails to Allay Higher-for-Longer Global Rates Threat
item.description: Governments around the world look to be facing lofty borrowing costs for at least the rest of the year, even as the fragile Middle East truce lowers energy prices and curbs inflationary fears.

item.title: China’s Finance Chiefs Take Center Stage as Economy Stalls
item.description: China’s central bank chief and top financial policymakers will take the stage Wednesday for the Lujiazui Forum, a gathering that could signal Beijing’s next moves to support the sputtering economy.

item.title: Japan’s Trade Balance Swings to Deficit as Yen Inflates Imports
item.description: Japan’s trade balance swung to a deficit for the first time in four months as the weak yen inflated the value of imports, even as volumes fell.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as US, Iran Prepare to Sign Interim Peace Deal
item.description: Gold held gains, as the US and Iran prepared to sign an interim peace deal that may ease global inflationary pressures arising from the war.

item.title: China Stock Gauge Sinks as Traders Favor AI Winners Elsewhere
item.description: Chinese stocks listed in Hong Kong are facing bleak milestones as a global rush into artificial intelligence supply chain players sidelines the Internet and consumer companies that dominate the offshore benchmark.

item.title: Laopu Gold’s Searing Rally Cools as Sales Slip, Bullion Falls
item.description: Slowing sales, a slump in its share price and a selloff in the gold market are fueling concerns that Laopu Gold Co., one of China’s hottest consumer brands, is losing momentum.

item.title: SoftBank Scales Back Latin America Tech Bets as Venture Capital Boom Fades
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is struggling to find startups in Latin America ready for major investments, underscoring how sharply the tech boom there has cooled since attracting record levels of venture capital only a few years ago.

item.title: Warsh Faces First Big Test as Fed Chair
item.description: Federal Reserve policymakers are expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday, posing an early test for new chairman Kevin Warsh as rising inflation erodes households’ purchasing power and President Donald Trump continues to press for lower borrowing costs.

item.title: Kalshi Aims to Expand Perpetual Futures After $5.5 Billion Debut
item.description: Kalshi Inc. is looking to expand its new perpetual futures business beyond digital assets after the never-expiring derivatives racked up more than $5.5 billion of trading volume in their first two weeks on the prediction-market platform.

item.title: Brookfield-Backed Data Center Firm Csquare Files for US IPO
item.description: Csquare Inc., a data center company backed by Brookfield Corp., filed publicly for a US initial public offering, joining a rush of debuts linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Slip Ahead of Fed, Oil Steadies: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia looked set to snap a three-day winning streak, tracking declines on Wall Street as investors rotated out of technology shares and positioned for the first Federal Reserve policy decision under Chairman Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Oil Holds Near Three-Month Low as Iran Deal Seen Boosting Supply
item.description: Oil held near a three-month low on expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will unleash a wave of supply.

item.title: Chile Central Bank Holds Key Rate at 4.5% as Fuel Price Shock Appears Contained
item.description: Chile’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged for the fourth straight meeting as economic activity flounders while the fallout of this year’s massive fuel cost hike remains contained.

item.title: Why Restoring Normal Traffic Through Hormuz Won’t Be Easy
item.description: The US and Iran have committed to reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most important artery for shipping oil and natural gas, which has been largely blocked since the two countries went to war in February. However, returning traffic in the strait to prewar levels — if that day ever comes — presents significant challenges. The prediction market Kalshi assigns a 51% probability that traffic will return to normal before Aug. 1 and a 68% probability before Sept. 1.

item.title: Inside the Palace: How Prabowo’s Circle Is Unnerving Investors
item.description: Unpredictable state intervention under Indonesia’s president is turning the emerging-market darling into a global laggard.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Leaving Lasting Scars Across Asia
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, we discuss how war in Iran hit Asia especially hard and why structural economic problems are likely to persist, even with a peace deal in the works.

item.title: Pension Funds in Talks With Canada on Airport Deals, Caisse CEO Says
item.description: The head of Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec says Canadian pension funds are having active talks with the government about investing in the country’s airports.

item.title: Exxon to Supply LNG to Help South Africa Reduce Reliance on Coal
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. struck a preliminary deal to bring liquefied natural gas to South Africa, which will use the fuel to bolster its coal-reliant power grid, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bond Options Traders Split on Fed Rate Path as Warsh Era Begins
item.description: Options traders are increasingly divided over the Federal Reserve’s near-term rate path, with conflicting bets that span from cuts to various degrees of hikes over the coming months.

item.title: More Evidence of a Relevering US Economy
item.description: Households debt is gathering steam.

item.title: Amazon Faces Billions in Penalties From Potential FTC Ad Suit
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is facing a possible lawsuit from the US Federal Trade Commission that may lead to billions of dollars in civil penalties, over claims the e-commerce giant misled advertisers, according to people familiar with the matter.
